message:
feat(indexer): atomic swap publish for staged lexical index with retained-backup rollback

The previous publish path was remove_dir_all(old) then rename(staged → live),
which had two problems:

1. Race window: between the remove and the rename, searchers saw no
   index at all. A crash in that window left the repo with only the
   staged tree under a different name — recovery required manual
   intervention.
2. No rollback: if the rename succeeded but a subsequent sanity check
   (or any follow-up step) failed, the old index was already gone.

Use renameat2(RENAME_EXCHANGE) on Linux to atomically exchange the
live and staged paths in a single syscall. After the swap:

- The former-live directory (now at the staged path) is renamed into
  a dated backup under <index>/.publish-backups/ for post-incident
  inspection and rollback.
- If the post-swap retain step fails, we exchange back so the live
  path keeps pointing at the original directory, and surface a
  context-rich error describing the rollback.

Add helpers:
- recover_or_finalize_interrupted_lexical_publish_backup: on startup,
  inspect the backup dir and either complete or revert a partially-
  published swap from a previous crash.
- ensure_lexical_publish_backups_dir: create the retention dir.
- unique_lexical_publish_backup_path: dated+suffixed names to avoid
  collisions when publish runs close together.
- atomic_exchange_paths: thin renameat2 wrapper via a local extern
  declaration (RENAME_EXCHANGE = 0x2, AT_FDCWD = -100).
- sync_parent_directory: fsync the parent after publish so the swap
  survives a crash.

Non-Linux targets fall back to the prior rename-based path (the
RENAME_EXCHANGE flag is Linux-specific); the first-publish case where
no live index exists yet uses a simple rename.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
